query: I just added @typescript-eslint/strict and now eslint --fix keeps changing my code back and forth between two styles. Can you figure out which rules are fighting each other?
We spread vitest.configs.all into our flat config and I'm pretty sure some of those rules contradict each other. Can you audit the vitest rules for conflicts?
Check our eslint.config.mjs for any redundant or superseded rules — I want to clean up anything that's doing double duty
After enabling strictTypeCheckedOnly from typescript-eslint, non-nullable-type-assertion-style keeps auto-fixing to x! but then no-non-null-assertion flags it. Is this a circular fix loop?
Are any of our ESLint rules mutually exclusive? We inherited a big config from another team and I suspect some presets enable rules that demand opposite patterns
I spread both stylistic and strict presets from typescript-eslint. Which rules overlap or conflict between those two presets?
Our CI keeps failing on eslint because two rules want different import ordering. One sorts by member name, the other by source path. Find all rule pairs like this in our config
We have prefer-to-be-truthy and prefer-strict-boolean-matchers both enabled — do those conflict? Run an audit on all our test file ESLint rules
I'm getting eslint errors I can't fix because two rules demand opposite things. One wants as const assertions, the other bans all type assertions. Can you find what's going on in our eslint config?
Run eslint --fix on the entire packages directory and auto-fix all linting errors
Add the @typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises rule to our eslint config at error severity
Our TypeScript build is failing with TS2345 type errors in the auth module. Can you investigate?
Migrate our .eslintrc.json legacy config to the new ESLint flat config format
Write unit tests for the user authentication service in packages/auth/src/auth.service.ts
Set up Prettier and configure it to work alongside ESLint without conflicts — add prettier-plugin-organize-imports too
Create a new ESLint plugin with a custom rule that enforces our logging conventions across the monorepo
Review the PR at #142 for code quality issues and leave comments on anything that looks off
Our vitest tests are flaky — sometimes expect(fn).toHaveBeenCalledOnce() passes and sometimes it doesn't. Can you debug why?
Upgrade eslint from v8 to v9 and update all plugins to their flat-config-compatible versions
Can you explain what the @typescript-eslint/consistent-type-assertions rule does and when I should enable it?
